name: Natural Reserve of the Laguna de Gallocanta — circular from Daroca
name_original: Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Daroca
region_intro: Road cycling around Daroca offers varied terrain across the Iberian System in Aragon, Spain. The region features a mix of mountainous areas, river valleys, and expansive steppe plains. Cyclists can navigate through diverse landscapes, including holm oak forests and the unique Laguna de Gallocanta Nature Reserve. The area is characterized by its natural features and historical attractions, providing a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Daroca The most popular road cycling route is Gallocanta Circular Route from Daroca, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding natural landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Santa Cruz Villages Loop from Daroca, a moderate 42.3 miles (68.1 km) path. This route traverses through villages and offers scenic views of the Sierra de Santa Cruz. Local road cyclists also love the Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Sierra de Herrera from Daroca, a 69.9 miles (112.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Daroca is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and steppe plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Daroca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from a few reviews. Nearly 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Daroca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Daroca?
A: Road cycling around Daroca offers a diverse range of terrain within the Iberian System in Aragon, Spain. You'll encounter mountainous areas, river valleys, and expansive steppe plains. Routes often feature a mix of holm oak forests and open landscapes, providing varied scenery and challenges.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Daroca?
A: Yes, Daroca offers several challenging routes for experienced cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990748093/climb-to-the-hermitage-of-our-lady-of-the-sierra-de-herrera-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Sierra de Herrera from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 112.5 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja of Daroca – Alto de Codos loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, covering 86.6 km with over 1100 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling near Daroca?
A: The region boasts unique natural features. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, a distinctive spring where water continuously falls in drops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990757268/gallocanta-circular-route-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Circular Route from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Laguna de Gallocanta Nature Reserve, Western Europe's largest saltwater lagoon and a crucial stopover for migratory birds.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Daroca is rich in history. Many routes offer glimpses of its well-preserved medieval architecture, including the impressive defensive walls and castles. You can pass through the emblematic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt; itself with its narrow streets and historic sites.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Daroca?
A: Yes, Daroca offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990757268/gallocanta-circular-route-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Circular Route from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990743827/natural-reserve-of-the-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Laguna de Gallocanta — circular from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; provides another opportunity to explore the natural reserve in a loop.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Daroca?
A: The best time for road cycling in Daroca is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter. This allows for comfortable rides and optimal enjoyment of the diverse landscapes.

Q: How long do road cycling routes around Daroca typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Daroca varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990757268/gallocanta-circular-route-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Circular Route from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; (48.6 km) can take around 4 hours, while longer and more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990748093/climb-to-the-hermitage-of-our-lady-of-the-sierra-de-herrera-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Sierra de Herrera from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; (112.5 km), may require over 5 hours.

Q: Can I cycle along the historic Camino del Cid near Daroca?
A: Yes, Daroca is a significant point on the historic Camino del Cid. This route offers both road and gravel cycling options, immersing riders in medieval Spain. While specific road cycling routes for the entire Camino del Cid are extensive, you can find stages and sections that traverse the area around Daroca, allowing you to experience this historic trail.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Daroca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from mountain ranges to steppe plains, and the opportunity to combine natural beauty with historical exploration. The mix of moderate and challenging routes also caters to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, you can explore charming traditional villages on your road cycling adventures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989961596/sierra-de-santa-cruz-villages-loop-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Cruz Villages Loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 68.1 km route specifically designed to traverse through villages, offering scenic views of the Sierra de Santa Cruz and a glimpse into local life.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Daroca?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Daroca, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include a good mix of moderate and difficult options, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes offer moderate challenges, beginners can find suitable options by choosing shorter sections of the moderate routes or focusing on routes with less elevation gain. The region has 13 moderate routes, which can be a good starting point for those looking for less strenuous rides compared to the more difficult climbs.
tips: The fact of being a border city between regions and kingdoms has marked the history of one of the most suffered but at the same time beautiful cities in Aragon. At this point we find ourselves at the Lower Gate that welcomes us to the city through which we can tour its walls, built at different times, up to the old castle from which we can enjoy an impressive panoramic view of Daroca.
Small, peaceful village next to Gallocanta.

Gallocanta and its lagoon is one of the most important nesting sites for migratory birds in southern Europe. The cranes, for example, are a very famous species in this area where they take advantage of the waters of this protected space to live.
